output:
Here’s a simple, budget-friendly, 7-day, gluten-free, lactose-free, onion-free meal plan based on your likes (meat, potatoes, Mexican, Thai, Indian), avoiding shellfish, heavy salt or sugar, and minimizing bitter greens for a super-taster palate. Each day = breakfast, lunch, dinner. Adjust portions for your appetite.

Day 1  
 Breakfast  
  • Scrambled eggs (2–3) with chopped tomato & baby spinach (small handful)  
  • 1 slice gluten-free toast, light olive oil or avocado spread  
 Lunch  
  • Grilled chicken salad: mixed lettuce, cucumber, tomato, shredded carrot, sliced grilled chicken thigh, olive oil + lemon dressing  
 Dinner  
  • Beef “taco bowl”: sauté lean ground beef with garlic, cumin, paprika; serve over lettuce, black beans (canned, rinsed), corn kernels, diced tomato, avocado, squeeze of lime  

Day 2  
 Breakfast  
  • Potato-pepper hash: diced potato + red bell pepper sautéed in olive oil & garlic, topped with a fried egg  
 Lunch  
  • Red lentil soup: red lentils + diced carrot, celery, garlic + ground cumin simmered in water or stock; serve with 1 slice gluten-free toast  
 Dinner  
  • Thai green chicken curry: chicken pieces simmered in light coconut milk with garlic, grated ginger, green curry paste (check for onion-free), frozen peas & carrot slices; serve with jasmine rice  

Day 3  
 Breakfast  
  • Banana–peanut butter smoothie: 1 banana, 1 Tbsp peanut butter, almond milk, ice (optional)  
 Lunch  
  • Turkey-avocado lettuce wraps: deli turkey (GF), avocado slices, tomato, shredded lettuce, a dash of lime  
 Dinner  
  • Chana masala (onion-free): canned chickpeas, canned tomatoes, garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, coriander, a pinch of asafoetida; serve with basmati rice  

Day 4  
 Breakfast  
  • Baked sweet potato (medium), split and topped with 2 scrambled eggs & chopped chives  
 Lunch  
  • Beef-stuffed bell peppers: halved bell peppers filled with cooked ground beef + cooked rice + canned tomato + garlic + paprika; bake ~20 min  
 Dinner  
  • Chicken fajitas: strips of chicken breast + sliced red & green peppers + garlic + chili powder sautéed; serve in warm corn tortillas with salsa (tomato, cilantro, jalapeño, lime)  

Day 5  
 Breakfast  
  • Gluten-free banana pancakes: mashed banana + 1 egg + 2 Tbsp GF oats or GF flour, pan-fry in minimal oil; top with fresh berries or yogurt alternative  
 Lunch  
  • Tuna-celery salad: canned tuna in water, chopped celery, diced cucumber, mayo (or avocado mash), lemon; eat in lettuce cups or on GF crackers  
 Dinner  
  • Chicken tikka masala (onion-free): chicken pieces marinated in yogurt-alternative (coconut yogurt), garlic, ginger, spices; simmer in canned tomato + coconut milk; serve with rice  

Day 6  
 Breakfast  
  • Omelette with diced tomato & red bell pepper, sprinkled with chopped parsley  
 Lunch  
  • Beef & veggie stir-fry: thinly sliced beef, broccoli florets, carrot sticks, garlic, tamari (GF soy sauce), serve with rice or rice noodles  
 Dinner  
  • Turkey chili over baked potato: ground turkey + canned kidney beans + canned tomato + garlic + chili powder; spoon over a baked white potato  

Day 7  
 Breakfast  
  • Avocado on gluten-free toast, plus 1 hard-boiled egg  
 Lunch  
  • Potato-egg salad: boiled potato cubes + chopped hard-boiled egg + diced celery + mayo or avocado oil mayo + fresh dill or parsley  
 Dinner  
  • Mexican-style stuffed sweet potatoes: bake sweet potato, top with leftover turkey chili or seasoned ground beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cilantro  

Snacks (optional each day)  
  • Fresh fruit (apple, banana)  
  • Carrot/cucumber sticks + hummus (onion-free)  
  • Handful of nuts or pumpkin seeds  

Tips  
  • Use garlic and fresh herbs generously to boost flavor without salt.  
  • Substitute any meat for what’s on sale (e.g. pork mince for beef).  
  • Prep double batches of soups, chili or curry to eat as next-day lunches.  
  • Check labels: buy certified gluten-free oats, tamari, tortilla chips, etc.  
  • For Indian dishes, a pinch of asafoetida (hing) adds “oniony” depth without using onion.  

This plan should keep costs down, prep time under 30 min per meal, and respect all your dietary restrictions and taste-bud sensitivities. Enjoy!

Here’s a 7-day, gluten-free, lactose-free, shellfish-free meal plan that steers clear of onions, keeps salt and sugar moderate, leans on meat, potatoes and your favourite cuisines, and uses simple, budget-friendly ingredients.  Where you’d normally use onion you can omit it or—if you tolerate it—add a pinch of asafoetida (hing) for Indian dishes.  Garlic is included where helpful.  Prep once, eat twice when you can; swap lunches and dinners around if you like.

Day 1  
Breakfast  
 • GF rolled oats cooked in almond milk, stirred in 1 tsp chia seeds and ½ sliced banana  
Lunch  
 • Grilled chicken breast (120 g), sliced over mixed lettuce, cucumber, tomato; dress with 1 Tbsp olive oil + lemon juice  
Dinner  
 • Beef & potato curry  
   – Sauté 200 g lean beef mince with 2 cloves garlic, 1" grated ginger, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp chili flakes  
   – Add 2 diced potatoes, 1 tomato (chopped), 200 mL water; simmer 20 min.  
   – Serve over boiled rice (⅔ cup cooked)  
Snack  
 • Carrot sticks + hummus (chickpeas, tahini, garlic, lemon, salt to taste)

Day 2  
Breakfast  
 • 2 scrambled eggs with a handful baby spinach  
Lunch  
 • Mexican chicken bowl  
   – Leftover grilled chicken (120 g), ½ cup cooked rice, ¼ cup corn kernels, diced capsicum, fresh salsa (tomato, coriander, lime)  
Dinner  
 • Thai-style fried rice  
   – Sauté 1 beaten egg, remove. In same pan, 120 g diced chicken, 1 cup mixed veg (carrot, capsicum, beans), 1 ½ cups cooked rice, 1 Tbsp gluten-free soy sauce, a handful Thai basil (if available). Return egg.  
Snack  
 • Apple slices + 1 Tbsp peanut butter

Day 3  
Breakfast  
 • Green smoothie: 1 banana, handful spinach, 1 Tbsp peanut butter, 200 mL almond milk  
Lunch  
 • Beef tacos  
   – Brown 150 g beef mince with ½ tsp each cumin & paprika, pinch chili  
   – Serve in 2 corn tortillas with shredded lettuce, tomato, dairy-free cheese  
Dinner  
 • Potato & chickpea “dal”  
   – Sauté 1 tsp mustard seeds in oil, add 2 cloves garlic, 1" ginger, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander powder  
   – Stir in 1 cup cooked chickpeas, 2 diced potatoes, 200 mL water; simmer until tender  
   – Serve with rice or gluten-free flatbread  
Snack  
 • 2 rice cakes topped with mashed avocado

Day 4  
Breakfast  
 • 2 slices gluten-free bread, mashed avocado, 1 poached egg  
Lunch  
 • Chicken & veg stir-fry  
   – 120 g chicken strips, sliced carrot & bok choy, 1 Tbsp GF soy sauce, 1 tsp grated ginger  
   – Serve over ½ cup cooked rice  
Dinner  
 • Mexican stuffed capsicums  
   – Halve 2 capsicums, fill with 150 g beef mince cooked with minced garlic, cumin, tomato paste, a splash water  
   – Top with dairy-free cheese, bake 15 min at 180 °C  
Snack  
 • Cucumber slices + guacamole (mashed avocado, lime, tomato, salt)

Day 5  
Breakfast  
 • Buckwheat pancakes (buckwheat flour, egg, almond milk) topped with mixed berries  
Lunch  
 • Tuna lettuce wraps  
   – 1 can tuna in spring water, drained, mixed with 1 Tbsp mayo, diced celery, pepper; wrap in lettuce leaves  
Dinner  
 • Thai peanut chicken  
   – Marinate 120 g chicken in 1 Tbsp peanut butter, 1 Tbsp GF soy sauce, garlic; stir-fry, serve over rice noodles  
Snack  
 • Handful mixed nuts

Day 6  
Breakfast  
 • Egg muffins (whisk 3 eggs + handful spinach + diced capsicum; pour into muffin tin; bake 15 min at 180 °C)  
Lunch  
 • Leftover beef & potato curry over rice  
Dinner  
 • Grilled steak (150 g)  
   – Season sparingly with salt & pepper  
   – Roast 2 chopped potatoes tossed in olive oil (200 °C, 25 min)  
   – Steam broccoli on side  
Snack  
 • A small bunch grapes

Day 7  
Breakfast  
 • Chia pudding (3 Tbsp chia, 200 mL almond milk, vanilla if you like) topped with berries  
Lunch  
 • Indian spiced red lentil soup  
   – Sauté 1 tsp mustard seeds, garlic, ginger; add 1 cup red lentils, 1 tsp turmeric, 4 cups water; simmer until thick.  
   – Season with salt & lemon; serve with rice or GF flatbread  
Dinner  
 • Chicken tikka salad  
   – Marinate 120 g chicken in dairy-free yogurt (e.g. coconut), 1 tsp garam masala, garlic, ginger; grill or bake  
   – Serve on greens with cucumber, tomato, lemon  
Snack  
 • Air-popped popcorn (light salt)

Weekly Staples Shopping List (approx.)  
 • Proteins: 1 kg chicken breast, 500 g lean beef mince, 2 steaks (or extra mince), dozen eggs, 1 can tuna, 1 can chickpeas, 1 cup red lentils  
 • Carbs: 1 kg rice, potatoes (1–2 kg), GF oats, buckwheat flour, GF bread or corn tortillas, rice noodles  
 • Veg & fruit: spinach, lettuce, tomato, cucumber, capsicum, carrot, broccoli, bok choy (or other greens), banana, mixed berries, apple, grapes  
 • Pantry: almond milk, chia seeds, peanut butter, mixed nuts, canned tomatoes, GF soy sauce, tahini, mayo, spices (turmeric, cumin, coriander, paprika, chili flakes, garam masala), garlic, ginger, olive oil  
 • Dairy-free cheese & yogurt

This plan yields simple, repeatable meals, minimal salty/sweet extremes, and plenty of flavour from Mexican, Thai and Indian cuisines—without gluten, lactose or shellfish. Enjoy!

Here’s a budget-friendly, gluten-free, lactose-free 7-day plan. Portion sizes can be adjusted to meet your energy needs (roughly 2,200–2,500 kcal/day for maintenance). Spices are kept at moderate levels to suit a “super‐taster” palate.  
   
Day 1  
Breakfast  
 • ½ cup gluten-free oats cooked in almond milk  
 • ½ banana, sliced  
 • Pinch cinnamon  
Lunch  
 • 150 g grilled chicken breast, seasoned with garlic powder, paprika  
 • 200 g roasted sweet potato wedges (olive oil, garlic powder, paprika)  
 • Steamed broccoli florets  
Dinner  
 • Turkey-and-bean chili (omit onion; use garlic, canned diced tomato, chili powder, cumin)  
 • ½ cup steamed rice  
Snack  
 • Apple slices with 1 Tbsp natural peanut butter  

Day 2  
Breakfast  
 • 2 scrambled eggs with baby spinach & diced tomato  
Lunch  
 • 2 corn tortillas  
 • 120 g lean ground beef, seasoned (cumin, garlic powder, mild chili)  
 • Lettuce, tomato, small handful dairy-free grated cheese  
 • Quick “no-onion” salsa (chopped tomato, cilantro, chopped jalapeño, lime juice, garlic)  
Dinner  
 • Thai chicken stir-fry: 150 g sliced chicken thigh, bell pepper, zucchini, garlic, ginger, 1 tsp fish sauce, lime  
 • ½ cup jasmine rice  
Snack  
 • Carrot sticks + homemade hummus (canned chickpeas, garlic, lemon juice, tahini)  

Day 3  
Breakfast  
 • Smoothie: 1 banana, ½ cup mixed berries, handful spinach, 1 cup almond milk, 1 Tbsp chia seeds  
Lunch  
 • Beef & potato hash: 150 g lean ground beef, diced potato, red bell pepper, garlic, paprika  
Dinner  
 • Aloo-chana (potato & chickpea curry): potato, canned chickpeas, tomato, garlic, ginger, garam masala, turmeric  
 • ½ cup basmati rice  
Snack  
 • Handful (30 g) raw almonds  

Day 4  
Breakfast  
 • 2 slices gluten-free toast with peanut butter & sliced banana  
Lunch  
 • Tuna salad: canned tuna (in water), cucumber, tomato, mixed lettuce, olive oil & lemon dressing  
Dinner  
 • Mexican rice bowl: ½ cup rice, black beans, corn kernels, grilled chicken strips, tomato, cilantro, lime  
Snack  
 • 1 orange  

Day 5  
Breakfast  
 • 2 hard-boiled eggs  
 • 1 slice gluten-free toast  
Lunch  
 • Thai beef stir-fry: 150 g beef strips, cabbage, carrot, garlic, ginger, 1 tsp fish sauce, lime  
 • ½ cup rice  
Dinner  
 • Beef & vegetable stew: stewing beef, carrot, potato, peas, tomato paste, garlic, mixed herbs  
Snack  
 • ½ cup coconut-based (dairy-free) yogurt + handful berries  

Day 6  
Breakfast  
 • ½ cup gluten-free oats with almond milk, 1 Tbsp chopped walnuts, cinnamon  
Lunch  
 • Chicken taco salad: mixed lettuce, shredded leftover chicken, corn, tomato, avocado, lime dressing  
Dinner  
 • Tandoori-style chicken: 150 g chicken thighs marinated in coconut-yogurt, garlic, ginger, tandoori spice  
 • Roasted potato cubes & steamed green beans  
Snack  
 • 1 pear  

Day 7  
Breakfast  
 • 2-egg omelet with spinach & tomato  
Lunch  
 • Leftover stew or curry from earlier in week  
Dinner  
 • Beef “nacho” bowl: seasoned ground beef, black beans, tomato, avocado, served over corn chips  
Snack  
 • 1 banana  

General tips  
 • Drink water, herbal tea or black coffee (no sugar).  
 • Buy meat in bulk/on sale, frozen veg and canned beans to save.  
 • Use the same staple (rice, potatoes, oats) throughout the week to keep costs down.  
 • Adjust portion sizes or add a small lactose-free protein shake if you need more calories.
